title: Volumes, PersistentVolumes, Claims, StorageClasses, CSI, Snapshots, and Recovery · Platform-System Defense
summary: A release review compares fresh volumes, persistentvolumes, claims, storageclasses, csi, snapshots, and recovery choices and accepts only answers tied to observable API, controller, workload, network, storage, or policy evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for stateful storage and recovery contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish image, writable layer, emptyDir, projected, hostPath, ephemeral CSI, persistent, and external application storage lifecycles and authority.
Trace PV and PVC capacity, access modes, volume mode, selectors, binding, claim references, node affinity, phases, expansion, and release.
Design StorageClasses with provisioner, parameters, defaulting, reclaim policy, expansion, mount options, and WaitForFirstConsumer topology behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz K8s App Consistent Backup Restore
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz StatefulSet loses a node while its PVC is bound in another zone, reclaim policy is Delete, expansion is pending, and the backup has never restored. Defend StorageClass, binding, access mode, topology, snapshot, restore, and application consistency. Competency probe: Define application quiescence, snapshot or logical backup, metadata, encryption, checksums, retention, cross-cluster restore, and changed-case validation.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz K8s App Consistent Backup Restore.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define application quiescence, snapshot or logical backup, metadata, encryption, checksums, retention, cross-cluster restore, and changed-case validation. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, keep K8s App Consistent Backup Restore connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A quiz StatefulSet loses a node while its PVC is bound in another zone, reclaim policy is Delete, expansion is pending, and the backup has never restored. Defend StorageClass, binding, access mode, topology, snapshot, restore, and application consistency. Competency probe: Define application quiescence, snapshot or logical backup, metadata, encryption, checksums, retention, cross-cluster restore, and changed-case validation.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the K8s App Consistent Backup Restore symptom.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Kubernetes Storage and CSI Documentation — Controls volumes, PVs, PVCs, StorageClasses, dynamic provisioning, topology, reclaim, expansion, CSI, snapshots, ownership, backup, and restore boundaries.

hints: For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s App Consistent Backup Restore precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A quiz StatefulSet loses a node while its PVC is bound in another zone, reclaim policy is Delete, expansion is pending, and the backup has never restored. Defend StorageClass, binding, access mode, topology, snapshot, restore, and application consistency. Competency probe: Define application quiescence, snapshot or logical backup, metadata, encryption, checksums, retention, cross-cluster restore, and changed-case validation.

For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A Bound PVC and successful VolumeSnapshot prove the application can recover within its objectives.

stimulus.title: K8s App Consistent Backup Restore failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A Bound PVC and successful VolumeSnapshot prove the application can recover within its objectives.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define application quiescence, snapshot or logical backup, metadata, encryption, checksums, retention, cross-cluster restore, and changed-case validation.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

For diagnose the hidden failure, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
